_meta.author: Claude Opus 4.8 (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates state-tier finalization, batch HD-47..51 (all R): Wren Williams (47), Eric Phillips (48), Madison Whittle (49), Tommy Wright (50), Eric Zehr (51). Evidence-first from primary roll calls (LIS vote-detail pages), Cardinal News crossover reporting, iVoterGuide candidate surveys, campaign sites, Freedom Index (Wright), and patroned-bill lists. All five voted the GOP line on BOTH the 2025/2026 reproductive-freedom and same-sex-marriage constitutional amendments; NONE among the Republican crossovers. No documented cross-party deviations found, so no FALSE cells — only documented-aligned TRUE cells are scored; every undocumented dimension left null (excluded from the denominator, not penalized). Corrections applied: Madison Whittle is MALE; Eric Zehr's district is Bedford/Campbell/Pittsylvania (seated Jan 2024, not a 2025 freshman); Phillips seated Jan 2024.

profile.religion: Protestant (official VA House profile); self-describes faith as a Christian whose 'faith guides my values'; church affiliation reported as Stuart Presbyterian Church (Patrick County) but not primary-source confirmed
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-47 (Carroll, Floyd, part of Henry, City of Galax — far SW Virginia); serving since 2022, re-elected Nov 2025. Attorney (co-owner, Schneider & Williams, Stuart). Hampden-Sydney College; J.D./MBA, Samford/Cumberland. Chairman of the Patrick County Republican Committee.

wren-williams.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-47 (far SW Virginia), R, since 2022; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier — va-legislature-refine autopilot] Consistent conservative record, no cross-party deviation found. HARD primary votes: NAY on the 2026 reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, 1/14/2026) and NAY on the 2026 same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3, 1/14/2026) — NOT among the 3 Republican HJ3 crossovers. Self-stated (iVoterGuide candidate survey): '100% Pro-Life,' marriage is 'one man and one woman,' opposes gender identity as a protected class, supports school choice incl. vouchers/charters/homeschooling, supports voter ID, 'Strongly Agree' the US should physically secure the southern border, 'unapologetically pro-gun.' Chief patron of HB781 (2022, 'divisive concepts'/CRT curriculum ban) and child-protection bills (HB148 child-exploitation property seizure; HB1556 AG authority over child sex-trafficking). Sponsored law-enforcement funding (HB1554 deputy sheriffs). Dimensions with no public record (christian liberty, economic specifics, 10th-Amendment, most election/border sub-cells) left null, not inferred. NRA/Giffords/ReproRising letter grades not located.

0.note: Voted NAY on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, 2nd reference, 1/14/2026); self-states '100% Pro-Life' and opposes taxpayer funding of abortion providers.
Voted NAY on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3, 2nd reference, 1/14/2026); self-states marriage is 'a God-ordained, sacred and legal union of one man and one woman.'

1.note: Voted NAY on codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3); not among the 3 Republican crossovers.

2.note: iVoterGuide candidate survey: opposes adding 'gender identity' as a protected class — affirms biological sex.

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Agree' with school choice including vouchers, tax credits, charter schools, and homeschooling; campaign backs local control and parental decision-making.

2.note: Chief patron of HB781 (2022) banning 'divisive concepts'/CRT-style content from public-school curricula.

1.note: Publicly supports voter-ID requirements; opposes 'voting without any form of ID.'

0.note: Sponsored law-enforcement support bills (HB1554, funding for deputy sheriffs, 2025; HB1453 first-responder protection, 2026).

2.note: Chief patron of child-exploitation property-seizure (HB148, 2026) and AG authority over child sex-trafficking cases (HB1556, 2025) — proportional punishment of evildoers.

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Agree' that the U.S. should do more to physically secure the southern border.

1.note: Self-states 'unapologetically pro-gun'; opposes letting victims sue firearm manufacturers/dealers; co-patroned/voted to roll back red-flag and one-handgun-a-month restrictions (Smith Mountain Eagle op-ed).

profile.religion: Christian (official VA House profile); his family founded Mercy Crossing Church in Henry County
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-48 (parts of Henry & Pittsylvania counties, City of Martinsville — SW Virginia); first seated Jan 2024 (special election), re-elected Nov 2025. Small-business owner (president/CEO, the Phillips Group); Lee University (History & Political Science).

eric-phillips.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-48 (Martinsville/Henry County), R, seated Jan 2024; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier — va-legislature-refine autopilot] Across-the-board conservative voting record from LIS roll calls (member H0384), no cross-party deviation. HARD: NAY on the reproductive-freedom amendment at every stage (HJ1/SJ247 2025; HJ1 2nd ref 2026) and NAY on the same-sex-marriage amendment at every stage (HJ9/SJ249 2025; HJ3 2026) — NOT among the Republican crossovers; NAY on statutory same-sex-marriage codification (HB174/SB101). YEA on religious accommodations at public higher-ed (HB131) and National Day of Prayer (SJ59). Consistent NAY on minimum-wage hikes; YEA on voter-ID (HB26) and NAY on absentee/mail-in expansions (machine-readable counting HB968, ballot-cure HB773/774). Tough-on-crime (fentanyl task force SB367; NAY on LE civilian-oversight disclosure). Anti-sanctuary: NAY on HB1441/SB783 (which would restrict local cooperation with federal immigration enforcement). 40+ NAY votes on gun-control (assault-weapons ban, red-flag/substantial-risk orders, storage mandates). AFP-VA endorsed. Self-stated 'unapologetically pro-life,' pro-2A. Undocumented dimensions (10th-Amendment, economic CBDC/sound-money specifics) left null.

0.note: Voted NAY on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment at every stage (HJ1 1/14/2025; SJ247 2/13/2025; HJ1 2nd reference 1/14/2026); self-states 'unapologetically pro-life,' 'life begins at conception.'
Voted NAY on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment at every stage (HJ9/SJ249 2025; HJ3 2026); not among the Republican crossovers.

1.note: Also voted NAY on statutory same-sex-marriage codification (HB174/SB101, 2024) — opposes same-sex marriage in law.

2.note: Voted NAY on the 2026 Democratic History/Social-Science SOL curriculum-framework revisions (HB333, HB614); campaign: 'classrooms are not a place for politics,' opposes 'woke legislators.'

1.note: Voted YEA on HB131 (2026), reasonable accommodations for religious observance at public higher-education institutions.

3.note: Voted YEA on SJ59 (2026) National Day of Prayer resolution and HB2302 (2025) tax exemption for religious buildings.

Voted NAY on absentee/mail-in expansions: satellite absentee offices (HB1490/HB2056), ballot-deadline extensions, absentee-ballot cure process (HB773/HB774), machine-readable absentee counting (HB968).

0.note: Pro-law-enforcement posture: NAY on LE civilian-oversight disclosure (HB1476) and surveillance-tech reporting mandates; YEA on retired-LEO info protection (HB1874/SB781) and LEO PTSD workers' comp (HB1313).

2.note: YEA on fentanyl enforcement (SB367 Fentanyl & Heroin Enforcement Task Force; SB1278/HB2742) and expanded larceny-prosecution venue (HB1256) — proportional punishment.
Voted NAY on HB1441/SB783 (2026), which would limit local law-enforcement cooperation with federal immigration authorities — an anti-sanctuary / pro-enforcement position.

1.note: 40+ NAY votes on gun-control across 2024-2026: assault-weapons ban (HB2/SB2, HB1607, HB217), waiting periods, red-flag/substantial-risk orders, secure-storage mandates, firearm-industry civil liability.

profile.religion: Christian; founding member of the Crossroads Christian Counseling Mission and active in local relief ministries; attended Sacred Heart Catholic School (Danville). Specific current denomination not formally confirmed.
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-49 (City of Danville + parts of Pittsylvania & Halifax — Southside Virginia); MALE (Madison John Redd Whittle). Seated Jan 14, 2026, succeeding retiring Republican Danny Marshall; previously Danville City Council (2016-2026). Small-business owner (property management/development, MJRW Inc.).

madison-whittle.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-49 (Danville/Southside), R; MALE; seated Jan 2026 after a decade on Danville City Council. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier — va-legislature-refine autopilot] Despite being a freshman, has a full first-session (2026) roll-call record (LIS member H0401). Consistent conservative voting, no cross-party deviation. HARD: NAY on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1/HB781/SB449/SJ1) and on contraception-mandate bills (HB6, SB596); NAY on the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ3/HB612/SB311/SJ3). YEA on religious accommodations (HB131) and National Day of Prayer (SJ59). NAY on the Democratic SOL curriculum revisions (HB333/HB614); YEA on SB778 (display of obscene material to a minor unlawful). NAY on absentee/mail-in expansions (HB773 cure, HB968). Tough-on-crime: NAY on bond release of felony arrestees (HB357), earned sentence credits (HB361), revocation-sentence limits (HB1413). Anti-sanctuary: NAY on HB1441/SB783; on Danville Council 'opposed sanctuary cities.' Pro-2A: NAY on the full gun-control slate (assault-weapons/magazine ban HB217, red-flag HB1096/HB896, storage mandates). Campaign: 'supporting law enforcement officers and first responders.' Undocumented dimensions left null. No third-party advocacy grades published yet (freshman).

0.note: Voted NAY on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, plus HB781/SB449/SJ1) and on contraception-access mandate bills (HB6, SB596) — consistent pro-life voting.
Voted NAY on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3, plus HB612/SB311/SJ3).

1.note: Voted NAY on every vehicle of the same-sex-marriage amendment — opposes same-sex marriage in the constitution.

2.note: Voted NAY on the 2026 Democratic History/Social-Science SOL curriculum-framework revisions (HB333, HB614).

3.note: Voted YEA on SB778 (2026) making display of obscene material to a minor unlawful with a penalty — protecting minors from sexualized content.

1.note: Voted YEA on HB131 (2026), reasonable accommodations for religious beliefs at public higher-education institutions.

3.note: Voted YEA on SJ59 (2026) National Day of Prayer resolution.
Voted NAY on absentee/mail-in expansions: absentee-ballot cure process (HB773), machine-readable absentee counting (HB968), voter-challenge changes (HB640).

0.note: Campaign: 'supporting law enforcement officers and first responders'; record of 'standing up for the safety of our citizens' on Danville City Council.

1.note: Anti-leniency: NAY on bond release of felony arrestees (HB357) and on earned-sentence-credit expansion (HB361).

2.note: NAY on limiting the sentence upon probation revocation (HB1413) — supports proportional punishment.
Voted NAY on HB1441/SB783 (limiting local cooperation with federal immigration enforcement); on Danville City Council 'opposed sanctuary cities' (his own statement).

1.note: NAY on the full 2026 gun-control slate: assault-firearms & large-capacity-magazine ban (HB217), red-flag/substantial-risk reporting (HB1096/HB896), storage mandates, firearm-industry liability (HB21/SB27).

profile.religion: No specific personal denomination publicly confirmed; has patroned numerous resolutions commending Baptist and Methodist congregations in his district
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-50 (Lunenburg/Southside Virginia); in the House continuously since Jan 2001 (District 61 2001-2024, renumbered to 50 in 2024 redistricting), re-elected Nov 2025. ~25 years' tenure. Grocer; Old Dominion University (political science); former Lunenburg County Board of Supervisors (chair).

tommy-wright.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-50 (Lunenburg/Southside), R, ~25-year incumbent (since 2001, formerly HD-61). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier — va-legislature-refine autopilot] Consistent down-the-line Southside conservative, no cross-party deviation. HARD primary: NAY on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1, party-line, 2026) and NOT among the Republican crossovers on the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9 2025 / HJ3 2026) — i.e., NAY. Own newspaper columns: opposes raising the minimum wage, criticizes the digital-services sales-tax hike, and condemns Gov. Spanberger signing the assault-weapons bill HB217 ('a direct attack on law-abiding gun owners'). Votes reported by the conservative Freedom Index (lifetime 79%, 92% 2024-25): YES on a CRT curriculum ban (HB787) and Family Life Education transparency (HB789); YES on HB775 'Religious Freedom'; NO on contraceptive mandates and on ranked-choice voting; YES on HB2193 'Defend the Guard' (a 10th-Amendment Guard-deployment measure that passed the House 99-0 — note the unanimity weakens it as a partisan signal). Listed on the Virginia Society for Human Life roster. Several dimensions (public justice, most election/border sub-cells, economic CBDC/sound-money) had no Wright-specific public record and are left null; ReproRising/NRA/Giffords letter grades not located.

0.note: Voted NAY on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, 2026, party-line with no Republican support); NO on contraceptive-coverage mandates (HB2371/HB609 per Freedom Index).
NOT among the 3 Republicans who crossed over on the 2026 same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ3) — i.e., voted NAY; also NAY on HJ9 (2025).

1.note: Long record opposing same-sex marriage in law; also voted in 2020 against repealing the statute banning sex before marriage.

2.note: Voted YES on a Critical Race Theory curriculum ban (HB787) and on Family Life Education curriculum transparency for parents (HB789), per the Freedom Index roll-call record.

1.note: Voted YES on HB775 'Religious Freedom' (protecting the exercise of worship from government emergency orders).

0.note: Voted YES on HB2193 'Defend the Guard' Act (bars Guard deployment to active combat absent a congressional declaration of war — a 10th-Amendment/anti-commandeering measure). NOTE: passed the House 99-0, so this is a documented aligned vote but not a distinctive partisan signal.

1.note: Own column condemning Gov. Spanberger's signing of HB217 (assault-weapons measure) as 'a direct attack on law-abiding gun owners and a deliberate challenge to the Second Amendment'; urges Virginians to buy now.

profile.religion: Member of Providence Church, Lynchburg (per his campaign bio); Liberty University graduate. (An unverified claim that he is an OPC elder was checked against the Lynchburg OPC officer page and not substantiated.)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-51 (parts of Bedford, Campbell & Pittsylvania counties — Central VA / Lynchburg area); first seated Jan 2024 (won 2023 general), re-elected Nov 2025. Small farmer / small-business owner (American House Wash, Rustburg); Liberty University (B.A. Psychology, M.A. Counseling Psychology); former Campbell County Board of Supervisors.

eric-zehr.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-51 (Bedford/Campbell/Pittsylvania — Lynchburg area), R, seated Jan 2024; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier — va-legislature-refine autopilot] Uniformly conservative, no cross-party deviation. HARD primary roll calls: NAY on the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247, 2/13/2025) and NAY on the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9, 1/14/2025). Patroned bills (Republican slate, mostly failed in the Democratic-majority House): HR454/HR28 reproductive-freedom study counter-resolutions; HB2146 medical-malpractice liability around minor gender-transition procedures; HB2388 voter-roll cleanup, HB2390 absentee-ID, HB2478 absentee-ballot drop-off regulation; HB2145 firearm ID, HB1230 arming trained school staff, HB1235 concealed-carry/protective-orders. Campaign positions: defend marriage as 'one man, one woman' and reverse Obergefell; prevent transgender bathroom access in Campbell County schools; oppose CRT in schools; mandatory voter ID and shortening early voting / ending vote harvesting; campus carry at Liberty University and eliminating concealed-carry fees. Bob Good-endorsed. NOTE: several campaign-cited bill numbers (HB1176/1180/1198/1206) do not match his actual patroned bills and were treated as SOFT positions, not scored as votes. Christian-liberty, public-justice, border, and economic-specific dimensions left null for lack of a clean record. No third-party advocacy grades located.

0.note: Voted NAY on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247, House floor 2/13/2025); patroned counter-resolutions HR454/HR28; campaign supports prohibiting tax-funded abortions and 'values life protection as a core belief.'
Voted NAY on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ9, House floor 1/14/2025); campaign 'led the fight to pass a resolution defending marriage as one man, one woman.'

1.note: Voted NAY on the constitutional amendment repealing the same-sex-marriage ban — opposes same-sex marriage in law.

2.note: Chief patron of HB2146 (2025) creating medical-malpractice liability around minor gender-transition procedures; campaign records leading efforts to prevent transgender bathroom access in Campbell County schools — affirms biological sex.
Campaign: opposes 'divisive critical race theory in schools' and sexually explicit materials in school libraries.

1.note: Campaign backs mandatory voter ID and patroned voter-roll-cleanup legislation (HB2388, 2025).

3.note: Chief patron of HB2478 (2025) regulating absentee-ballot drop-off locations; campaign supports shortening the 45-day early-voting window to 14 days and ending 'vote harvesting.'

1.note: Chief patron of multiple pro-Second-Amendment bills (HB2145 firearm ID without photo; HB1230 arming trained school staff; HB1235 concealed-carry/protective orders); campaign backs Liberty University campus carry and eliminating concealed-carry fees.
